steps.title: Identify the key requirement
steps.content: The task asks the student to “make and support a claim.” This means stating a position about human arrival in North America backed by specific evidence from the notes. The notes document multiple pre-Clovis sites that contradict the Clovis First hypothesis.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content: A makes the claim (“the Clovis were likely not the first humans”) and backs it with evidence (“items from more than a dozen sites suggest humans had arrived before that time”)—exactly what a supported claim requires. B asserts a position without naming evidence, C merely restates the hypothesis, and D references a single site vaguely. That matches choice A — A is correct.
